content.full_text:
June 12, 1990

Mr. Scott Hallabrin  
Acting General Council  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, California 95804

RE: Use of PAC Funds

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

Pursuant to several telephone conversations with staff council Blanca Breeze, this request is being made in an effort to gain clarification on the statutes which govern PAC accounts. The California Association of Management (CAM) hereby requests advice relative to the following questions.

CAM is a 501(c)(3) organization which represents over 400 managerial, supervisory and confidential civil service employees throughout the state of California. The Association conducts educational seminars and conferences and provides other services for the benefit of its members. In addition, it monitors selected items of legislation of interest to management employees in State service. In this regard, CAM has maintained a political action committee account for several years. In the past, the funds have been selectively used to contribute to legislative fundraisers. However, in the last two years the account has been relatively inactive.

The organization is now at a point where the funds in the PAC account are needed to meet 1990-91 budget shortfalls within the general fund account. CAM would like to use all or part of its PAC funds in any one or more of the following ways:

1. To pay for legislative monitoring/advocacy services such as a monthly retainer, bill service and other related expenses.
2. To carry forward CAM's program of educational seminars for its members.
3. Complete or partial transfer of the PAC funds into the Association's general fund account.

We would appreciate your prompt attention to this request. Please feel free to contact me with any additional questions at the above telephone number. We anxiously await your response.

Sincerely,

Angela Brown
Director of Membership Services

AB:kmh
